observations.recommendation:
1.1. We recommended the following actions, and Management agreed that:
A. Road Networks
a. The Municipal Treasurer:
1. As the General Service Officer (GSO) of the LGU, develop and maintain a comprehensive Road Map to accurately determine all Road Lots owned by the Municipality;
2. Submit the updated Road Map to the Municipal Engineer as a basis for measuring Road Lots;
b. The Municipal Engineer:
3. Upon receipt of the Road Map, prepare and submit a detailed report specifying the dimensions and areas of all Road Lots, both existing and newly developed, whether paved or unpaved;
4. Provide this report to the Municipal Assessor for valuation;
c. The Municipal Assessor:
5. Using the updated Road Map and dimensional data, determine the assess value of all Road Lots, categorized as paved and unpaved, based on the newly updated Provincial Ordinance No. ZN-19-182 from the SP;
6. Submit an official valuation report to the Municipal Accountant for financial recording;
d. The Municipal Accountant:
7. Record the Road Lots at its fair market values through the preparation of a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV);
8. Maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card (LRNLC) to track and monitor Road Lot valuations; and
9. Prepare an adjusting entry to recognize initially the assessed value of Road Lots as a separate component of the Road Network System, using the following suggested entry:
Dr.: Road Network
P91,495,156.32
Cr.: Prior Period Adjustment
P91,495,156.32
B. The CIP – Buildings and Other Structures account
1. Continuously retrieve documents related to the recorded CIP beginning balance;
2. Provide the CIP data to the Municipal Engineer for inspection of its existence and completion, upon identifying the details of the CIP; and
3. Draw a JEV to effect the erroneous entry in the CIP account in the amount of P0.599 million.
C. Tangible items below the capitalization threshold
a. Conducting a thorough review of JEV and its supporting documents to ensure proper application of capitalization criteria; and
b. Drawing a JEV to effect the necessary adjustments to correct and/or restate the balances of PPE, and other affected accounts, to fairly present the financial statements as of year-end.
D. Delayed reclassification of completed CIP and inaccurate calculation of depreciations
a. Conduct a comprehensive review, recalculation, and revision of the lapsing schedule for the depreciation of the Road Network Systems, Water Supply Systems, Other Structures, and other related unsampled PPE accounts to ensure accuracy and compliance with accounting standards;
b. Implement the timely reclassification of CIP accounts by recognizing the transfer to their respective PPE accounts at the period they become available for use, thereby ensuring the proper and accurate recognition of depreciation; and
c. Prepare and record the necessary adjusting entries to correct the understatement of accumulated depreciation and depreciation expense for the Road Network Systems, Water Supply Systems, and Other Structures.
E. Upgrade of vehicle
We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to properly review the transactions and capitalize the cost incurred for major spare parts, upgrade and stand-by equipment which is expected to be used for more than one year and that can only be used with an item of PPE.

2.1. We recommended, and the Management agreed the following:
a. The HRMO, during budget preparations of the Annual Budget, to include not only the retirable/co-terminous employees and elected officials during the Annual Budget period but also those employees/officials who will retire and whose term of service will end in the ensuing year in the computation of the terminal leave benefits;
b. The Local Finance Committee (LFC) to prioritize the allocation of leave benefits computed by the HRMO in the Annual Budget; and
c. The Municipal Accountant to:
1. Recognize in the books of accounts the unrecorded leave benefits payable and subsequently, the leave benefits which are expected to be settled within 12months after the end of the reporting period, subject for availability of appropriation and funds certified by the Municipal Budget Officer and Treasurer. The proposed adjusting entries are as follows:
Dr: Prior Period Adjustments
P8,801,617.29
Cr.:Leave Benefits Payable
P8,801,617.29
To recognize prior years’ accumulated earned leave credits as of December 31, 2024.
Suggested accounting entries upon payment:
Dr.:Leave Benefits Payable
Dr.:Terminal Leave Benefits*
Dr.:Prior Period Adjustments**
Cr.:Cash in Bank
To record payment of accumulated earned leave credits
*Leave credits earned during CY 2025
**PYs’leavebenefitsduetoincreaseofsalaryin CY 2025;
2. Ensure compliance with IPSAS 1 and 20 by providing the required disclosures in the Notes to the Financial Statements, specifically on the aggregate renumeration of key management and those who are already retired for the year and those that are retiring in the next reporting period, as well as the Municipality’s adoption of IPSAS 39 in accordance with COA Circular No. 2024-009; and
3. Provide a disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements the nature of the leave benefits payable and its expected payment for at least the following year.

1.1. We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. Prepare JEVs to correct the misstatements of Inventory and Expenses accounts upon submission of SSMI with RIS prepared by the GSO and Distribution List by the end users/requisitioning offices; Proposed adjusting entries are as follows:
Dr.:Prior Period Adjustment
P4,341,745.00
Cr.:Other Supplies and Materials for Distribution
P4,341,745.00

P1,000,000.00

P1,000,000.00
b. Draw a JEV to recognize the inventory and accounts payable in the books upon receipt of the AIR and DR from GSO.
1.2. Moreover, the GSO agreed to:
c. Furnish the Municipal Accountant advance copies of AIR and DR after inspection and delivery and
Prepare SSMI to consolidate all RIS and submit them to the Municipal Accountant weekly.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to properly review the transactions and classify each expense related to trainings, tangible items, membership insurance and goods for distribution in their appropriate Expenses accounts, in accordance with the prescribed RCA LGU.

1.1. We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Assessor and Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
a. Reconcile their records to establish the correct balance of total unpaid taxes as of December 31, 2024 and adjust the discrepancies accordingly; and
b. Conductregularreconciliationbetweentheir respective records to ensure timely detection of errors in posting/entries, if any.
Also, we recommended, and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to furnish the Municipal Accountant the certified list of delinquent taxpayers with the corresponding unpaid taxes due at the beginning of the year to ensure that the RPT/SET Receivablesset-upbytheMunicipal Accountant is reliable.

1.1. We recommended, and the concerned officers agreed to implement the following measures:
a. Non-conduct of Physical Inventory-taking for the Local Roads
b. Non-maintenanceoftheRoadMapby the GSO
c. Non-creation of Inventory Committee
d. Non-preparation of cost segregation and a complete description report for each road network components
e. Non-preparation of the LRNLC
a. The Municipal Accountant
Establish and maintain LRNLCs for each component of the local road network system to ensure proper monitoring, reconciliation, and reporting of road infrastructure assets;
Prepare and regularly update subsidiary records for roads and road components for each road network, ensuring consistency with the General Ledger and the Local Road Inventory; and
Develop a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation per road component to ensure accurate financial reporting and proper valuation of public infrastructure assets.
b. The Municipal Treasurer/ GSO
Conduct and maintain a Local Road Inventory, ensuring that all road components, including road lots, pavements, drainage systems, and other structures, are properly recorded and accounted for;
Secure a printed and regularly update the Road Map from the MPDC, which has been prepared using the QGIS. This map should be updated periodically to reflect actual and existing road networks for effective planning, maintenance, and decision-making; and
Establish and maintain a LRNPC, providing a comprehensive record of all roads and their components to facilitate asset tracking and accountability.
c. The Municipal Engineer
PrepareandsubmitadetailedreportontheRoad Network System, including a complete description of each road and a cost segregation report that itemizes the value of each road component to ensure accurate financial reporting and asset management; and
Provide the above report to the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer/GSO for proper documentation and integration into financial records.
Additionally, we recommended, and the LCE agreed to formally create a dedicated Inventory Committee exclusively responsible for conducting the physical inventory of the Road Network System

1.1. We recommended, and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
a. Intensify collection of delinquent taxes on real property by frequently notifying delinquent taxpayersthroughissuanceofnoticeandregular update of posting of Notices of Delinquency in the LGU bulletin board and conspicuous places in each barangay concerned; and
Prioritize the conduct of IEC on RPT to educate the taxpayers of the importance and benefits of paying their respective tax dues on time and encourage delinquent taxpayers to avail the tax amnesty on real property taxes and special levies on real property per Republic Act (RA) No. 12001 dated June 13, 2024

1.1. We recommended, and the Management agreed to direct the concerned Offices/employees to submit the corresponding supporting documents of the unpaid expenses during the year to the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant within the cut-off date to ensure that all lawful expenditures and obligations incurred during the year are taken up in the accounts of that year.
Moreover, the Management agreed to refrain from using the current appropriations to pay unpaid expenses of the preceding year, in accordance with Section 305(a) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended, and the Management agreed that:
The Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of physical inventory of PPE and prepare the RPCPPE;
The GSO to prepare the IIRUP based on the RPCPPE, using the prescribed format for all PPE found unserviceable, obsolete and/or no longer needed, and submit to the Disposal Committee for appraisal and disposal; and
The Disposal Committee, following the procedures set forth in COA-DBM JC No. 2024-1 to, upon receipt of the IIRUP, conduct appraisal and disposal of all unserviceable PPEs.

We recommended, and the Management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant to check the completeness ofsupporting documentsattached before certifying the DVs. These documents are important to establish whether the LGU is qualified for the grant of CNA Incentives, the savingsgeneratedwerethroughcostcuttingand systems improvement measures, and to determine who are entitled to CNA;
Ensure that the accomplishment report of PPAs, as of September 30, 2024, is at least an average of 75 per cent of all the targets under their PPAs approvedintheLGUbudgetforCY2024andthat the process of authorizing the supplemental budget is to be followed; and
The Municipal Budget Officer to include only those available unobligated balances of allowable MOOE enumerated under item 4.3.1 of the DBM Circular No. 2024-4, as afund sourceof the CNA Incentives.

We recommended, and the Management agreed that:
The MDRRM Officer to utilize the 5 per cent LDRRMF appropriations accordingly;
Refrain from charging against the 5 per cent LDRRMF the operational and administrative expenses of the MDRRM Office; and
The MDDRM Officer propose budget to the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Budget Officer to increase the operational and administrative expenses of the Office under the General Fund. Otherwise, limit its expenditures upotheappropriationsprovidedfortheMDRRM Office.

We recommended, and the Management agreed to:
Demand the contractor to install the lacking items enumerated above and rectify the structural defects including fixing the doors and other broken items, since the project is still under the warranty period of one year;
The Municipal Engineer and the members of the Inspectorate Team to sign the Certificate of Inspection only if the implementation and completion thereof strictly adhere to the approved plans and specifications; and
Subsequently, require the contractor/s to submit as built plans before final payment for all infrastructure projects

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to orient the Special Disbursing Officer/end-user/implementing office on the proper procurement process, the required documents to be attached in the liquidation documents, and ensure that goods and services are not procured directly from the supplier without undergoing the procurement process and procedures enumerated under Annex H of the RIRR of RA 9184.
Also, we recommended, and the Special Disbursing Officer/Requisitioning Officer agreed to submit first the approved PR with the BAC and payment is to be made only when the procurement process and procedures are completed, and goods are received/delivered.

1.1. We recommended, and the BAC agreed to:
a. Ensure to conduct other alternative procedures in luring out other responsive bidderstogeneratewidercompetitioninorderto obtain the most advantageous price to the LGU; and
b. Craft policies and sanctions to prevent nuisance/uninterested bidders that may undermine or even rigged the result of the procurement process

1.1. We recommended, and the GFPS in coordination with the MPDC agreed to:
a. Update the GAD Database by conducting gender analysis to identify gender issues/gaps and determine gender-responsive PPAs for prioritization in programming and budgeting in the annual GPB in accordance with Annex B of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by JMC No. 2016-01; and
b. Ensure that proposed GAD PPAs of departments for allocation are supported by program profile containing the identified gender issues/GAD mandates and gender analysis situation of the community.

1.1. We recommended, and the LSB agreed to:
a. Submit the quarterly SEF Utilization report to DepEd CO, and furnish a copy to the DBM IX and DILG IX not later than 20th day after the end of the quarter and for the annual report not later than the 15th day of February of the following year; and
b. Post the quarterly SEF Utilization report on the three identified conspicuous places not later than the 20th day after the end of the quarter.

1. Inventory committee to formulate a detailed PIP containing the specific duties and assignment of its members from start until the targeted final completion date to be able to come up and submit the complete RPCPPE and other required output following the specific guidelines and procedures for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU; prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed format; and reconcile the RPCPPE with the Property Division/Unit and Accounting Unit records as provided under COA Circular 2020-006 and that the reconciliation shall be completed within ten (10) days from rendition of the RPCPPE by the Inventory Committee;

2. Accounting Unit to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if any, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; and

3. Property unit to issue demand letters to those accountable officers for those missing PPEs established during the physical count and cause the conduct of an investigation to determine who are responsible for the loss, among others.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE enjoins the following department heads’ compliance with the requirements of the Circular: 

4. The Municipal Accountant to record the road lot component, prepare the LRNLC, keep and maintain subsidiary records for each component of the road network, prepare a monthly lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component except for the road lot and review the details of the account and establish the correct amount of Road Network account to bring harmony between various reports submitted.

5. The Municipal Treasurer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, keep a complete LRNPC for all roads and its components, at the end of the accounting period, continuously maintain a RLRN for each road network component and reconcile records received from the Municipal Engineer, Inventory Committee and its record on file; and

6. The Municipal Accountant reconcile its records with the GSO/Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the:

13. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to secure copies of documents and other pertinent records of the donated assets in order to support the recording in the books of account of the Municipal Accountant;

14. Inventory Committee to include these items in the inventory count and subsequently provide property stickers and property cards for monitoring of custody and accountability; and

15. Create Appraisal Committee and require the latter to appraise the donated PPEs to its current market value in case documents for its measurement are not successfully obtained from the donating entity.

18. We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the unremitted contributions and to regularly remit the monthly premiums and loan amortization of individual employees as provided in the above cited regulations

22. We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure complete documentation before payment is made to the payee in consonance with pertinent rules and regulations.

28. We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to fully institutionalize the LDRRMO by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions to assist the LDRRM Officer in the administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

31. We recommended and Management agreed to obtain approval from the source agency for the utilization of the unexpended balances for another PPA. Otherwise, require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the return of the unused funds of the completed PPAs to the source agency, in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013.

We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to:

32. Fast-track the segregation of solid waste to avoid the risk of pollution that may affect public health and safety and educate the garbage collectors and those in charge in the dump site to segregate the collected waste;  

33. Fast-track the approval of their application for Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC) for the Construction of Sanitary Landfill; and

34. Negotiate with neighboring LGU who have functional sanitary landfill to help them manage their waste through a Memorandum of Agreement.

35. We recommended and Management agreed to comply with Section 37 of RA No. 9003 to ensure protection of public health and environment by closing open dump site.

36. We reiterated our recommendation and Management agreed to impose sanctions and penalties for violators based on the approved local ordinance. Likewise, regularly conduct information and education campaign for the general publics’ awareness on environmental compliance.

We reiterated our recommendations and Management agreed to:

38.  Conduct gender audit thru the use of GMEF and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the LGU in terms of GAD compliance; and

39.  Equip the GFPS with the in-depth knowledge and capacity on GAD by undergoing/attending trainings and seminars such as those relating to gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and the use of various GAD tools for the LGU’s GAD implementation of PAPs.

40. Management to issue demand letters to those with outstanding advances for its full settlement and liquidations; and for those dormant accounts involving ten (10) years and above, require the filing of request for authority to write-off with the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to make an adjusting entry as follows:

Direct the Municipal Engineer to timely submit to the Municipal Accountant completion and turn-over documents to record the completed PPE in the books.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the:
Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry to reverse the entry made for the DRRMF – MF - CO amounting to P400,525.00 and to reflect only the unexpended balance of the QRF and the DRRMF – MOOE; and
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer of the unexpended balance of the current DRRMF composed of QRF and MOOE in the GF to Special SF and moving forward, to transfer the unexpended current DRRMF - QRF and MOOE in cash at year-end.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the Municipal accountant to reclassify the balance of Due to NGA account to Due to GOCC account:
Due to NGA    P3,311,946.01
Due to GOCC     P3,311,946.01

Moving forward, we recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant to strictly follow the accrual basis of accounting in recording transactions and ensure that only those obligated but delivered items are recognize and recorded as assets/expense and payable as at year-end; and
The Municipal Budget Officer to take up obligations only when supported with PO or contracts.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Treasurer prepare the ITR and submit to the Municipal Accountant following the prescribed procedures in COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022; and
The Municipal Accountant upon receipt of the original copy of the ITR and other supporting documents from the Municipal Treasurer, prepare a JEV to derecognize the transfer and forward a copy of the ITR to the barangays for recording in their books of accounts.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reflect the remaining inventory of drugs and medicines purchased in CY 2023 and make prior period adjustment since the book of accounts was already closed; and
Record the receipt of Drugs and Medicines thru Inventory account supported by AIR, in compliance with the perpetual inventory method and record only as expense upon issuance duly supported by SSMI with RIS and distribution lists.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Treasurer shall prepare RIS upon issuances of accountable forms, and regularly prepare the SSMI summarizing all the issuances made and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books; and
The Municipal Accountant recognize issued accountable forms as expense upon receipt of the SSMI and RIS from the Municipal Treasurer.

Therefore, in view of the accrual basis of accounting, this Office recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Health Officer (MHO) to regularly communicate and submit to the Municipal Accountant a Monthly List of Reimbursable Claims filed with PHIC; and
The Municipal Accountant to:
Record in the GF books of accounts the PHIC reimbursable claims from PHIC upon receipt of the List from the RHU by a debit to the Due from GOCC - PHIC account and credit to Revenue accounts and refrain from accounting the receipt of payments from PHIC as TF; and
Make representation to the Philhealth that the LGU shall follow the accounting principle in recognizing revenue under IPSAS 9 on the amount received from Philhealth for services rendered to patients with Philhealth coverage

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Treasurer shall furnish RPT Account Register with accurate and complete information to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of each year; and
Upon receipt of the RPT Account Register, the Municipal Accountant shall draw a JEV to set-up the RPT /SET Receivables and its Deferred RPT/SET, in its respective books of accounts.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct:
The inventory committee to:
Formulate a detailed Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing the specific duties and assignment of its members from start up to its targeted final completion date to be able to come up with and submit the complete RPCPPE and other required output following the specific guidelines and procedures for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU;
Prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed format; and
Reconcile the RPCPPE with the Property Division/Unit and Accounting Unit records. As provided under COA Circular 2020-006, the reconciliation shall be completed within ten (10) days from rendition of the RPCPPE by the Inventory Committee.
The Accounting Unit to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if any, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
Instruct the property unit to issue demand letters to those accountable officers for those missing PPEs established during the physical count and cause the conduct of an investigation to determine who are responsible for the loss, among others.
We recommended, and Management agreed that the Municipal Budget Officer record the adjustments of the obligated disbursements in the RAAO.

We recommended, and Management agreed:
Ensure to strictly follow the procurement procedures and ensure that deliveries are covered by PO; and
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to properly review the request for procurement of goods and ensure that appropriate mode of procurement is recommended and carried out in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its implementing rules and regulations.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Cease the practice on charging ineligible expenditures from LDRRMF; and
Allocate a separate budget in the GF for the LDRRMO intended for its operational expenses
We recommended, and Management agreed that the BAC to send invitation to observers to include other stages of the procurement process as mentioned in Section 13.1 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.
We recommended, and Management agreed that the BAC shall invite observers at least five calendar days before the procurement stage or activity.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the General Service Office and/or Municipal Treasurer furnish COA copies of AIR together with sales invoice/delivery receipts, copy of contract/purchase order and other necessary documents within twenty-four hours after acceptance of deliveries of goods and services.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the BAC to strictly follow the procurement procedures prescribed in RA No 9184 and its RIRR and exercise due diligence in ensuring that award of contract shall only be made to suppliers with complete eligibility documents submitted during opening of bids.

We recommended, and Management agreed to direct the BAC to ensure that requests for the procurement of goods and services shall be made thru public bidding unless alternative mode of procurement is warranted and allowable under conditions stated in Section 48 of RA No. 9184 and strictly observe and proper procedures in the conduct of procurement in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR.

We recommended, and Management agreed that moving forward, the BAC Secretariat shall prepare a BAC Resolution recommending the approval to the HoPE the award of the contract to the supplier with the lowest calculated and responsive quotation before the issuance of POs.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the BAC TWG to properly conduct the post-qualification procedures and document the same by preparing a post-qualification report to show that the lowest/single calculated bidder is technically, legally, and financially capable and is responsive to the bid.
We recommended, and Management agreed that the BAC Secretariat to post all the NOA and NTP as required by Sections 37 and 54 of the RIRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The personnel assigned to deliver the PO to the contracting supplier to ensure that the document is dated when it was signed as to “CONFORME’; and
The Municipal Treasurer, as the designated GSO to ensure that PO is complete as to the required information.

We recommended, and Management agreed to create and establish M&E Team in consonance with the provision Section 5 of the PCW DILG DBM NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to evaluate the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the GAD Focal Point System thru the LCE endorse to the Committee on Family, Women, Children, and Elderly of the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) the passing of an ordinance localizing RA No. 11313 and post the same in conspicuous places. Further, we recommend that the ordinance include provisions on the following: a) measures to prevent gender-based sexual harassment in educational institutions; b) imposition of fines/penalty for violation thereof; and c) creation of sexual harassment hotlines.

We recommended, and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer to:
Intensify collection of delinquent real property taxes by frequently notifying delinquent taxpayers through posting of Notices of Delinquency as required by law, and regular conduct IEC on RPT payment in collaboration with the barangay officials; and
Regularly send List of RPT Delinquency to the Provincial Treasurer for information and enforcement of civil remedies specified in the Local Government Code.

We recommended, and Management agreed that:
The Municipal Accountant to prepare the JEV upon receipt of paid DVs and continuously communicate with the Municipal Treasurer to submit the DVs and its supporting documents within the prescribed period; and
The Municipal Treasurer to regularly notify the payees of their checks to ensure timely release from their Office and submission to the Municipal Accountant of paid DVs.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

7. Prepare a JEV to prepare adjusting entries to correct the Other Payables, Government Equity, Due to Other Funds and the Trust Liability – LDRRMF; and

8. Adhere to the provisions including the illustrative accounting entries contained in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

16. Inform the payee about the stale check, request for the return of the stale check, mark the stale check cancelled on its face, issue a replacement check, and report it in the RCI; and

17. Regularly send written notification to the payee of the existence of the check one month prior to it becoming stale.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the management:
Local Chief Executive (LCE) to relieve the personnel who were designated as Inventory Committee members of their present and regular duties to devote their full time in preparing the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) containing the specific duties and assignment of its members from start up to its targeted final completion date to be able to come up with and submit the complete RPCPPE and other required output following the specific guidelines and procedures for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU, instruct the Inventory Committee to reconcile the RPCPPE with the Property Division/Unit and Accounting Unit records. As provided under COA Circular 2020-006, the reconciliation shall be completed within ten days from rendition of the RPCPPE by the Inventory Committee, instruct the Accounting Unit to prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if any, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006, instruct the property unit to issue demand letters to those accountable officers for those missing PPEs established during the physical count, and cause the conduct of an investigation to determine who are responsible for the loss, among others; and
MT and MA to maintain proper and complete property card and ledger cards, respectively, for easy determination, identification, and reconciliation of records.

We recommended that Management direct the MT to remit intact the accumulated taxes withheld from compensation and its suppliers/contractors within the prescribed periods. Any penalty and surcharges that may be imposed by the BIR is at the expense of the persons causing the delayed remittance.

We recommended that Management direct the preparers of the accounting entries in the JEV to ensure the correct accounts to be used in recording transactions and direct the MA to review the entries made the former;

We recommended that Management direct the MA to prepare and adjusting entry to correct the amount of the accounts Other Payables and the Trust Liability – DRRMF with the following entry:
GF:
Other Payable 				P3,685,055.23
Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account 		P3,685,055.23
TF:
Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account 	P3,685,055.23
Trust Liability – DRRMF					    P3,685,055.23
Moving forward, we also recommended that Management direct the MA to adhere to the provisions including the illustrative accounting entries contained in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the management to strengthen the internal controls over collections and deposits by requiring the MT to review the RCD, update the cash book regularly, and monitor the Cash in Local Treasury balance before depositing the collections to the authorized government depository bank.
We recommended that the management to provide personal protective equipment to all its collectors and personnel directly dealing with solid waste as required under RA No. 9003.

We reiterated our recommendation that management to fast-track the segregation of solid waste to avoid the risk of pollution that may affect public health and safety and educate the garbage collectors and those in charge in the dump site to segregate the collected waste.  Also, since there is already a closure order issued by the DENR, management should have safe closure and rehabilitation project or program of the old site.
We recommended fast-tracking the approval of their application for ECC for the Construction of Sanitary Landfill.

We reiterated our recommendation that the municipality to impose sanctions and penalties for violators based on the approved local ordinance. Likewise, information and education campaign to be regularly conducted for the general publics’ awareness on environmental compliance.

We reiterated our recommendation that the concerned offices be required to implement a monitoring system and control measures necessary for the timely transmittal of documents to allow earlier preparation of reports and timely submission to this Office.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management:
To conduct gender mainstreaming through gender audit and gender analysis of its policies, programs, services and the current situation of the LGU in terms of GAD compliance;
To equip the GFPS with the in-depth knowledge and capacity on GAD by undergoing/attending trainings and seminars such as those relating to gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender analysis, gender audit and the use of various GAD tools for the LGU’s GAD implementation of PAPs; and
To revisit and strictly comply with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9710 and other related provisions of law.
We recommended that Management direct the LDRRMO to ensure that PAPs to be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF in previous years in the TF will be included in the revised LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that Management direct the LDRRMO to submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to the COA Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and LDC.

We reiterated our recommendation that management to fully institutionalize the LDRRMO by filling up the statutory/mandatory positions to assist the LDRRM Officer in the administration and training and research and planning to assure implementation of actions and measures related to disaster risk reduction and management.

We recommended that Management direct the MA to ensure complete documentation of every transaction and adhere to the rules and regulations as provided above before payment is made to the payee in consonance with pertinent rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendations that:
Management stops the practice of charging against the 20 per cent DF the expenditures which do not have direct contribution to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development; and
Management strictly observes the guidelines provided by the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20 per cent DF.

We recommended that Management ensure that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) carry out their mandated functions, particularly in recommending the use of alternative mode applicable in the circumstances, to get the lowest possible prices obtaining in the market taking into account the principles of transparency, competitiveness, accountability and equal opportunities of legitimate suppliers to participate in government procurement. Henceforth, management to observe religious compliance the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its implementing rules and regulations.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipality enhance its compliance with policies and procedures in:
Completing the actual physical count and the RPCPPE and reconcile the established differences to have accurate Fixed Asset records and reports for the Municipality for which it is held accountable and provide adequate disclosures to guide the users of information;
Maintaining proper Fixed Asset records for all Municipality’s fixed assets;
Assigning Fixed Asset to end user or the least the head of office having custody of the assets to facilitate easy monitoring for inventory purposes and ensure that the same are being kept and safeguarded by responsible personnel
Updating information in the Fixed Asset records;
Coordinating with different personnel/offices of the Municipality in order to gather information as to the amount of acquisition cost and the date of acquisition to be able to recognized depreciation expense.
If no information is available, since in the actual physical count of these assets they are classified as serviceable, proper appraisal on the remaining useful life of the asset should be done and the corresponding depreciation expense shall be computed based on the  book – value as presented in the financial statements.
Management Comment:
The Municipality informed that they are in the process of completing the physical count and come up with the inventory report. The Municipality further informed that they are in the process of reconciling records to effect adjustments.
They further explained that acknowledgment receipts of property were updated to determine who is responsible for the safekeeping of the asset.

The Municipality agree with the findings and gave assurance to implement the same, and acknowledged that they will increase their budgetary allocation to comply with the 1% requirements.
